evals.prompt: I'm building an agent runtime and need to implement timeouts for child processes. I want a 5-minute overall timeout, but also a 30-second no-output timeout to catch hung processes. How should these two timeout mechanisms work independently vs together?
evals.expected_output: Should explain both timeoutMs and noOutputTimeoutMs as independent mechanisms, touchOutput() resets noOutputTimeoutMs, and both can be set simultaneously but operate separately

evals.prompt: My agent runtime spawns child processes that can fork their own children. When I kill a parent process, the children keep running. How should I implement process tree termination on Linux and Windows?
evals.expected_output: Should explain Unix kill(-pid, SIGTERM) for process group with grace period, Windows taskkill /T, and the force-kill fallback after grace period

evals.prompt: I'm implementing PTY support and need to know how to properly close stdin on both Unix and Windows. What's the correct EOF character for each platform?
evals.expected_output: Should explain Unix uses \x04 (Ctrl+D) and Windows uses \x1a (Ctrl+Z), and why this difference exists

evals.prompt: A process exits with code 124. What does this convention mean and when should an agent runtime produce this exit code?
evals.expected_output: Should explain 124 is the standard timeout exit code (same as GNU timeout), produced when timeoutMs fires and process exited with code 0

evals.prompt: I'm getting EINVAL errors when spawning npm scripts on Windows. What's happening and how should I handle Windows-specific spawn issues for .cmd/.bat files?
evals.expected_output: Should explain CVE-2024-27980, wrapping batch files with cmd.exe /d /s /c, and npm/npx shim to avoid direct .cmd spawn

evals.prompt: How should I design the adapter interface so that child mode and PTY mode can be used interchangeably by the supervisor?
evals.expected_output: Should explain the common SpawnProcessAdapter interface with pid, stdin, onStdout, onStderr, wait(), kill(), dispose() methods

evals.prompt: My PTY sessions are becoming zombies when I kill them - the wait() promise never resolves. How should I prevent this?
evals.expected_output: Should explain force-kill fallback timer (4 seconds) that resolves the wait promise even if PTY doesn't emit onExit
